blurb:
If you haven't been to Jerusalem Bakery yet, you're missing out on one of the better deals in Marietta. The prices are low, the food is legit, and the shawarma alone is worth a detour. My wife and I started going here when we wanted a quick lunch that didn't feel like fast food, and it's become a regular stop.

The shawarma is what most people go for, and they're right to. The falafel is also solid, and they've got enough veggie options that it works even if you're with someone who doesn't eat meat. If you've got a sweet tooth, stick around for the desserts.

Good for a casual weeknight dinner or a grab-and-go lunch. Nothing pretentious about it. Just good food at a price that won't make you wince.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
Marietta Square Market is a food hall, which means you're not committing to one thing when you walk in. Twenty vendors under one roof, a bunch of different cuisines, and everyone in your group gets to eat what they actually want. My wife and I go here when we can't agree on a restaurant and don't feel like negotiating. Problem solved.

The variety is the whole appeal. Different stalls, different styles, something for pretty much every mood. Walk around, see what looks good, grab a seat. It's a low-pressure way to eat well.

This one is great for a casual night out, a group with picky eaters, or just browsing around the Square area. Open until 10pm on weekends, which gives you some flexibility.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
This place is a Marietta institution. Open 24 hours, every day, which sounds like a gimmick until you're out late on a Friday and just need a real meal. My wife and I have ended up here more times than I can count, and honestly it never disappoints. The neon out front, the big booths, the menu that goes on forever -- it's the kind of place that just feels right no matter what time you walk in.

The menu is massive, but the Greek specialties are what set it apart from your average diner. And the breakfast is serious. Big portions, classic execution, nothing fancy. If you want eggs and a short stack at 2am or a full plate of spanakopita at noon, they've got you.

This one's good for pretty much any occasion -- solo lunch, group breakfast, late night after an event. The 24/7 thing is real and it's genuinely useful to have in your back pocket.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours

blurb:
This is the spot my wife drags me to when she wants to celebrate something. Brazilian all-you-can-eat barbecue, and they do it well. The setup is relaxed, not stuffy, which I appreciate. You're not overdressing for this one. Good for a group, good for date night, good for anyone who just wants to eat a lot of really good meat without much ceremony.

The cheese bread is famous for a reason. Order it, eat it, order more. The feijoada stew is a solid pick if you want something more traditional and hearty. The barbecue itself keeps coming around, which is the whole point.

They also serve breakfast and open at 10am daily, which is not something you'd expect from a steakhouse. Makes it more versatile than it looks on the surface.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
A 4.9 on almost 17,000 reviews. Let that sink in. Douceur De France is not flying under the radar and it's not hard to see why. My wife found this place first and now we're there more weekends than I'd like to admit. It's a French bakery doing baguettes, quiches, petits fours, and a full brunch spread, and they do all of it well. Bright, cheerful spot that makes you slow down a little.

The quiche is what I keep coming back to. Solid every time. My wife goes straight for the pastry case and has yet to have a bad pick. The baguettes are the real deal if you want to grab something to take home.

Just know they close at 6pm Monday through Saturday, and Sundays they wrap up at 3:30pm. Plan accordingly if you're thinking about a lazy Sunday brunch -- get there before it's gone.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA

restaurant_name: Crowne Plaza Atlanta Perimeter at Ravinia by IHG
blurb:
If you have out-of-town guests coming in and need a solid place to put them up near Perimeter, the Crowne Plaza at Ravinia is a reliable pick. It has an indoor pool, a gym, and a restaurant and bar on site, so they won't need to go far after a long travel day.

It's also worth knowing about if you're looking for a spot to grab a drink or a casual meal near the Perimeter area without a lot of fuss. The rating is strong for a hotel property, which usually means the basics are done right.
address: 4355 Ashford Dunwoody Rd, Atlanta, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
If you've been looking for a good biryani spot near Perimeter, this is the one locals keep coming back to. It's colorful, casual, and unfussy -- the kind of place where the food does the talking and nobody's trying to impress you with the decor. Good for a family dinner or a casual lunch when you want something with real flavor.

The biryani is obviously the main event here. It's served family-style, so it works well if you're bringing a group. The portions are solid for the price, and the traditional Indian dishes on the menu give you plenty of options beyond rice if you want to mix it up.

They open at 9am on weekends, which is a nice option if you want something different for a late breakfast or early lunch.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
Rreal Tacos at Sandy Springs has a 4.9 with nearly 10,000 reviews. That's not hype -- that's just people coming back and telling their friends. The place has good energy, the service is attentive, and it works for pretty much any occasion from a casual lunch to a late night out with friends. They're open until midnight most nights, which is handy around here.

Start with the Esquite if you want a flavor-packed opener. For drinks, reviewers rave about the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Rreal Passion Fruit pitcher -- both described as refreshing and well-balanced. Good call if you're coming with a group.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
A 4.9 with nearly 6,000 reviews is not an accident. Mister O1 has become a go-to for people around Sandy Springs who want real Italian thin-crust pizza, and it delivers. The vibe is relaxed enough for a weeknight dinner but good enough for when you have people visiting and want to take them somewhere you know won't let you down.

The pizza is the reason to come -- traditional thin crust with ingredients sourced from Italy or locally, depending on the item. The antipasti and salads are worth starting with before the pizza hits the table. It's the kind of spot where you end up back here a week later.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the place you take someone when you actually want to impress them. It sits right on the Chattahoochee, and the view alone makes it worth the drive to Powers Ferry. This is date-night territory, anniversary dinner territory, or the kind of place you bring family in town when you want to show off a little.

The Southern seafood is the reason to come. The menu leans into that Chattahoochee riverside feel, and the setting is intimate without being stuffy. With over 11,000 reviews at 4.6 stars, this place has been earning its reputation for a long time.

Heads up -- Sunday hours split into two seatings, with a break between 4pm and 5pm. Worth checking the website before you go if you're planning a Sunday evening.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
